scope: Apply ADR-108 doctrine refinements + re-run Sprint 31A matrix
doctrine_additions: Step B addendum: load_skill slug exactness (never path/prefix/filename)
Step C addendum: agent-loop semantics (N tool calls per message != parallel; use delegate per item)
New section: 'Act, don't describe' (after announcing intent, next message must be the tool call)

key_signals.comment: Same as 31A baseline — tool path works, content-match still rejected on Edit 4.
Fix 2 (agent-loop semantics) did NOT take on MiniMax — still 10x serial reads.
Fix 1 partial: 'review' not a path but still wrong slug. Fix 3 did NOT take.
BIG WIN: Fix 1 + Fix 2 both took. 0 -> 6 delegate calls, cost dropped $1.36 -> $0.52 (62%).
Fix 3 did NOT take on Claude either — still plans batches in prose without invoking the tool.
Claude NEW load_skill uptake (was 0 in 31A). Delegate count dropped 7->4 (partition strategy).

fix_1_slug_exactness.evidence: GPT-5.4-mini T2: load_skill(name='nda-review') canonical (was wrong path in 31A). Claude T2: NEW load_skill uptake.
fix_1_slug_exactness.partial_miss: GPT-5.4-mini T1: load_skill(name='review') — closer to slug shape, still wrong (no 'review' slug exists).

fix_2_agent_loop_semantics.evidence: GPT-5.4-mini T2: 0 -> 6 delegate calls. MiniMax T2: still 10x serial reads.
fix_2_agent_loop_semantics.interpretation: MiniMax delegate gap appears to be model-capability bound, not doctrine-bound.

fix_3_act_dont_describe.evidence: GPT-5.4-mini T1 + Claude T1: both still plan redline batches in prose without invoking redline__process_document_batch.
fix_3_act_dont_describe.diagnosis: Failure mode occurs at end of long multi-turn flow; doctrine in mid-prompt isn't reaching it. Sprint 33 candidate: move 'act, don't describe' into bespoke Commercial redline doctrine or into the tool description.
